title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement bblodfon@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

code: @Manual{,   title = {survdistr: Survival Distribution Container with Flexible Interpolation Methods},   author = {John Zobolas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.2.9000},   url = {https://survdistr.mlr-org.com}, }

what: Survival Distribution Container with Flexible Interpolation Methods
title: Survival Distribution Container with Flexible Interpolation Methods
text: Survival distribution container efficient storage, management, interpolation survival model predictions.

title: Survival Distribution Container with Flexible Interpolation Methods
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("mlr-org/survdistr")

title: Survival Distribution Container with Flexible Interpolation Methods
text: Linear interpolation survival matrix using survDistr R6 class: Interpolation Kaplan-Meier survival curve using exported R function calls C++ code:
code: library(survdistr)  # generate survival matrix mat = matrix(data = c(0.9,0.6,0.4,0.8,0.8,0.7), nrow = 2,              ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE) x = survDistr$new(x = mat, times = c(12, 34, 42), method = "linear_surv") x ## A [2 x 3] survival matrix ## Number of observations: 2 ## Number of time points: 3 ## Interpolation method: Piecewise Constant Density (Linear Survival) # stored survival matrix x$data() ##       12  34  42 ## [1,] 0.9 0.6 0.4 ## [2,] 0.8 0.8 0.7 # S(t) at requested time points (linear interpolation) x$survival(times = c(5, 30, 42, 50)) ##              5        30  42  50 ## [1,] 0.9583333 0.6545455 0.4 0.2 ## [2,] 0.9166667 0.8000000 0.7 0.6 # Cumulative hazard H(t) x$cumhazard(times = c(5, 42)) ##               5        42 ## [1,] 0.04255961 0.9162907 ## [2,] 0.08701138 0.3566749 # Probability density f(t) x$density(times = c(5, 30, 42)) ##                5         30     42 ## [1,] 0.008333333 0.01363636 0.0250 ## [2,] 0.016666667 0.00000000 0.0125 # Hazard h(t) x$hazard(times = c(5, 30, 42)) ##                5         30         42 ## [1,] 0.008695652 0.02083333 0.06250000 ## [2,] 0.018181818 0.00000000 0.01785714 library(survival)  fit = survfit(formula = Surv(time, status) ~ 1, data = veteran) tab = data.frame(time = fit$time, surv = fit$surv) head(tab) ##   time      surv ## 1    1 0.9854015 ## 2    2 0.9781022 ## 3    3 0.9708029 ## 4    4 0.9635036 ## 5    7 0.9416058 ## 6    8 0.9124088 tail(tab) ##     time        surv ## 96   411 0.045022553 ## 97   467 0.036018043 ## 98   553 0.027013532 ## 99   587 0.018009021 ## 100  991 0.009004511 ## 101  999 0.000000000 # constant S(t) interpolation interp(   x = tab$surv,   times = tab$time,   eval_times = c(0, 3.5, 995) ) ##           0         3.5         995  ## 1.000000000 0.970802920 0.009004511 # linear S(t) interpolation interp(   x = tab$surv,   times = tab$time,   eval_times = c(0, 3.5, 995),   method = "linear_surv" ) ##           0         3.5         995  ## 1.000000000 0.967153285 0.004502255 # exponential S(t) interpolation interp(   x = tab$surv,   times = tab$time,   eval_times = c(0, 3.5, 995),   method = "exp_surv" ) ##         0       3.5       995  ## 1.0000000 0.9671464 0.0000000

title: Coerce Object to survDistr — as_survDistr
code: as_survDistr(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' as_survDistr(   x,   times = NULL,   method = "const_surv",   check = TRUE,   trim_duplicates = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'survDistr' as_survDistr(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as_survDistr(x, ...)

title: Coerce Object to survDistr — as_survDistr
text: x Object convert. ... Additional arguments passed methods. times (numeric) Numeric vector time points corresponding columns x. NULL, column names x used. method (character(1)) Interpolation method passed survDistr constructor. check (logical(1)) Whether validate x times. trim_duplicates (logical(1)) Whether remove duplicate S(t) values corresponding time points.

title: Coerce Object to survDistr — as_survDistr
text: survDistr object.

what: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
text: Validates input proper probability matrix vector representing either survival function, cumulative distribution function (CDF), cumulative incidence function (CIF), discrete hazard, discrete density.

title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
code: assert_prob(x, times = NULL, type = "surv")

title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
text: x (numeric() | matrix()) Survival vector matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Original time points. NULL, extracted names/colnames. type (character(1)) Type probability function: "surv" (default), "cdf", "cif", "haz", "dens".

title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
text: Invisibly returns validated numeric time points.

title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
text: following conditions must hold: input x numeric matrix missing values. Time points (times) numeric, non-negative, unique, increasing. supplied, derived (col)names(x) (coerced numeric). values valid probabilities, .e. lie \([0,1]\). row monotone: "surv": non-increasing survival curves, .e. \(S(t_i) \ge S(t_{+1})\). "cdf" / "cif": non-decreasing functions, .e. \(F(t_i) \le F(t_{+1})\). "haz" / "dens": monotonicity requirement. Boundary condition t = 0: "surv": \(S(0) = 1\). "cdf" / "cif": \(F(0) = 0\). "haz" / "dens": \(t_1 > 0\) (otherwise, nonzero hazard/density t = 0 implies \(S(0) \neq 1\))

title: Assert probability matrix or vector — assert_prob
code: x = matrix(data = c(1, 0.6, 0.4,                     0.8, 0.8, 0.7),            nrow = 2, ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE)  # Explicitly provide time points assert_prob(x, times = c(12, 34, 42), type = "surv")  # Or use column names as time points colnames(x) = c(12, 34, 42) assert_prob(x)  # check CDF assert_prob(1 - x, type = "cdf")  # check discrete hazards assert_prob(c(0.2, 0.01, 0.3), times = c(1, 2, 3), type = "haz")  # check discrete densities assert_prob(c(0.2, 0.01, 0.3), times = c(1, 2, 3), type = "dens")

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
text: Converts density hazards one four input representations survival probabilities anchor time points (interpolation).

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
code: convert_to_surv(   x,   times = NULL,   input = "cont_haz",   check = TRUE,   clamp_surv = FALSE,   eps = 1e-12 )

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
text: x (numeric() | matrix()) Input vector matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Anchor time points. NULL, extracted names/colnames x. input (character(1)) Input type. One "disc_haz", "disc_dens", "cont_haz" "cont_dens". check (logical(1)) TRUE (default), run input validation checks. Disable know input valid want skip checks speed. clamp_surv (logical(1)) TRUE, clamp survival probabilities [eps, 1] avoid numerical issues. eps (numeric(1)) Small value used clamp near-zero survival probabilities clamp_surv = TRUE.

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
text: numeric vector matrix survival probabilities dimensions x.

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
text: Let \(t_1,\dots,t_B\) denote anchor time points, \(\Delta_j = t_j - t_{j-1}\), \(S_j = S(t_j)\) survival probabilities anchors. conversion depends value input: "disc_dens": \(S_j = 1 - \sum_{k=1}^j \tilde f_k\) "disc_haz": \(S_j = \prod_{k=1}^j (1 - \tilde h_k)\) "cont_dens": \(S_j = 1 - \sum_{k=1}^j f_k \Delta_k\) "cont_haz": \(S_j = \exp\!\left(-\sum_{k=1}^j \lambda_k \Delta_k\right)\)

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
text: check = TRUE, validate input proper discrete density/hazard matrix vector using assert_prob(). continuous hazards/densities, check input non-negative numeric matrix/vector.

title: Convert density/hazard to survival — convert_to_surv
code: # Continuous hazard => survival haz_cont = c(0.02, 0.1, 0.2, 0.15) times = c(0, 1, 2, 3) convert_to_surv(haz_cont, times = times, input = "cont_haz") #> [1] 1.0000000 0.9048374 0.7408182 0.6376282  # Discrete hazard => survival haz_disc = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.15) times = c(1, 2, 3) convert_to_surv(haz_disc, times = times, input = "disc_haz") #> [1] 0.900 0.720 0.612

what: Extract time points from a probability matrix or vector — extract_times
title: Extract time points from a probability matrix or vector — extract_times
text: Helper function consistently obtain validate time points associated probability matrix vector.

title: Extract time points from a probability matrix or vector — extract_times
code: extract_times(x, times = NULL)

title: Extract time points from a probability matrix or vector — extract_times
text: x (numeric() | matrix()) Probability vector (length = time points) matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Optional vector time points corresponding x.

title: Extract time points from a probability matrix or vector — extract_times
text: validated numeric vector time points.

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
text: Interpolates survival curves (vector matrix) new time points using internal C++ interpolation functions. Output can survival, cumulative distribution, density, hazard cumulative hazard functions.

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
code: interp(   x,   times = NULL,   eval_times = NULL,   method = "const_surv",   output = "surv",   add_times = TRUE,   check = TRUE,   eps = 1e-12,   trim_duplicates = FALSE )

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
text: x (numeric() | matrix()) Survival vector matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Anchor time points. NULL, extracted names/colnames. eval_times (numeric() | NULL) New time points interpolate. Values need unique, increasing non-negative numbers. NULL, anchor times used. method (character(1)) Interpolation method; one "const_surv" (default), "const_dens" (alias: "linear_surv") "const_haz" (alias: "exp_surv"). output (character(1)) Output type: "surv", "cdf", "cumhaz", "density" "hazard". add_times (logical(1)) TRUE, attach eval_times names/colnames. check (logical(1)) TRUE, run input matrix validation checks using assert_prob(); set FALSE skip checks (recommended external use). eps (numeric(1)) Small positive value used replace extremely low survival probabilities computing cumulative hazard, preventing numerical instability \(-\log S(t)\) calculations. trim_duplicates (logical(1)) TRUE, removes adjacent duplicate values input using trim_duplicates(). eliminates flat segments survival curves improves interpolation efficiency. Default FALSE.

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
text: numeric vector matrix interpolated values.

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
text: Input must always survival probabilities. currently provide three interpolation options: "const_surv": left-continuous constant interpolation S(t) (default). "const_dens"/"linear_surv": linear interpolation S(t) (equivalent piecewise constant interpolation density function). "const_haz"/"exp_surv": exponential interpolation S(t) (equivalent piecewise constant interpolation hazard function). formulas method, see respective Tables arxiv preprint (TODO: add link). constant hazard interpolation ("const_haz"), right-anchor S(t) values equal 0 internally floored min(1e-12, S_left) within interval. keeps hazards/densities finite without allowing local increase S(t).

title: Interpolate Survival Curves — interp
code: x = matrix(c(1, 0.8, 0.6,              1, 0.7, 0.4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) times = c(0, 8, 13) eval_times = c(5, 10, 14)  # constant S(t) interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times) #>      5  10  14 #> [1,] 1 0.8 0.6 #> [2,] 1 0.7 0.4  # linear S(t) interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "linear_surv") #>           5   10   14 #> [1,] 0.8750 0.72 0.56 #> [2,] 0.8125 0.58 0.34  # exponential S(t) interpolation (same as `method = "const_haz"`) interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "exp_surv") #>              5        10        14 #> [1,] 0.8698237 0.7130410 0.5664525 #> [2,] 0.8001774 0.5596066 0.3576452  # Cumulative distribution with linear S(t) interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "linear_surv", output = "cdf") #>           5   10   14 #> [1,] 0.1250 0.28 0.44 #> [2,] 0.1875 0.42 0.66  # H(t) with linear S(t) interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "linear_surv", output = "cumhaz") #>              5        10        14 #> [1,] 0.1335314 0.3285041 0.5798185 #> [2,] 0.2076394 0.5447272 1.0788097  # f(t) with constant hazard interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "const_haz", output = "density") #>               5         10         14 #> [1,] 0.02426194 0.04102582 0.03259165 #> [2,] 0.03567540 0.06263294 0.04002878  # h(t) with constant hazard interpolation interp(x, times, eval_times, method = "const_haz", output = "hazard") #>               5         10         14 #> [1,] 0.02789294 0.05753641 0.05753641 #> [2,] 0.04458437 0.11192316 0.11192316

title: Interpolate CIF matrix — interp_cif
text: Interpolates cumulative incidence (CIF) functions (corresponding one competing event ) using left-continuous constant interpolation.

title: Interpolate CIF matrix — interp_cif
code: interp_cif(x, times = NULL, eval_times = NULL, add_times = TRUE, check = TRUE)

title: Interpolate CIF matrix — interp_cif
text: x (matrix()) CIF matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Anchor time points. NULL, extracted colnames(x). eval_times (numeric() | NULL) New time points interpolate. Values need unique, increasing non-negative numbers. NULL, anchor times used. add_times (logical(1)) TRUE, attach eval_times colnames output matrix. check (logical(1)) TRUE, run input matrix validation checks using assert_prob(); set FALSE skip checks (recommended external use).

title: Interpolate CIF matrix — interp_cif
text: Interpolated CIF matrix.

title: Survival Distribution Container — survDistr
text: survDistr R6 specialized container designed storing managing prediction outputs survival models single-event settings. includes models Cox proportional hazards, random survival forests, classical machine learning-based survival estimators. main prediction data type survival matrix, rows represent observations columns represent time points.

title: Survival Distribution Container — survDistr
text: Key design features: survival matrix stored internally can accessed using $data() method. $times active field provides time points corresponding matrix columns. interpolation method controlled via $method active field. Survival-related quantities (e.g., distribution, density, hazard functions) interpolated using interp() function. assert_prob() function validates input data matrix construction check set TRUE. Use $filter() method subset observations -place filtering rows stored matrix. Use trim_duplicates = TRUE constructor remove flat survival segments (repeated values across time points) pre-specified tolerance (controlled pre-processing, see trim_duplicates()).

title: Survival Distribution Container — survDistr
text: times (numeric) Numeric vector time points corresponding columns data. Read-. method (character(1)) Interpolation method; one "const_surv" (default), "const_dens" (alias: "linear_surv") "const_haz" (alias: "exp_surv").

title: Survival Distribution Container — survDistr
text: survDistr$new() survDistr$print() survDistr$data() survDistr$filter() survDistr$survival() survDistr$distribution() survDistr$density() survDistr$cumhazard() survDistr$hazard() survDistr$clone()

text: x (matrix) numeric matrix survival probabilities (values 0 1). Column names must correspond time points times NULL. times (numeric(1)) Numeric vector time points matrix x, must match number columns. method (character(1)) Interpolation method; one "const_surv" (default), "const_dens" (alias: "linear_surv") "const_haz" (alias: "exp_surv"). check (logical(1)) TRUE, run input matrix validation checks using assert_prob(); set FALSE skip checks (recommended external use). trim_duplicates (logical(1)) TRUE, removes adjacent duplicate values input using trim_duplicates(). eliminates flat segments survival curves improves interpolation efficiency. Default FALSE.

title: Survival Distribution Container — survDistr
code: # generate survival matrix mat = matrix(data = c(1,0.6,0.4,0.8,0.8,0.7), nrow = 2,              ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE) times = c(12, 34, 42) x = survDistr$new(mat, times) x #> A [2 x 3] survival matrix #> Number of observations: 2 #> Number of time points: 3 #> Interpolation method: Piecewise Constant Survival   # stored survival matrix x$data() #>       12  34  42 #> [1,] 1.0 0.6 0.4 #> [2,] 0.8 0.8 0.7  # interpolation method x$method #> [1] "const_surv"  # time points x$times #> [1] 12 34 42  eval_times = c(10, 30, 42, 50) # S(t) at given time points (constant interpolation) x$survival(times = eval_times) #>      10  30  42  50 #> [1,]  1 1.0 0.4 0.4 #> [2,]  1 0.8 0.7 0.7 # same but with linear interpolation x$method = "linear_surv" x$survival(times = eval_times) #>             10        30  42  50 #> [1,] 1.0000000 0.6727273 0.4 0.2 #> [2,] 0.8333333 0.8000000 0.7 0.6  # Cumulative hazard x$cumhazard(times = eval_times) #>             10        30        42        50 #> [1,] 0.0000000 0.3964153 0.9162907 1.6094379 #> [2,] 0.1823216 0.2231436 0.3566749 0.5108256  # Density x$density(times = eval_times) #>              10         30     42     50 #> [1,] 0.00000000 0.01818182 0.0250 0.0250 #> [2,] 0.01666667 0.00000000 0.0125 0.0125  # Hazard x$hazard(times = eval_times) #>        10         30         42         50 #> [1,] 0.00 0.02702703 0.06250000 0.12500000 #> [2,] 0.02 0.00000000 0.01785714 0.02083333

title: Remove adjacent duplicate values — trim_duplicates
text: Removes adjacent duplicate values time axis, possibly probability vector matrix (e.g. survival curves). Equality determined numeric tolerance. matrices, duplicate detection done column-wise across rows. earliest time point run (near-)equal values kept.

title: Remove adjacent duplicate values — trim_duplicates
code: trim_duplicates(x, times = NULL, tol = 1e-10)

title: Remove adjacent duplicate values — trim_duplicates
text: x (numeric() | matrix()) Vector (length = time points) matrix (rows = observations, columns = time points). times (numeric() | NULL) Optional time points corresponding x. NULL, extracted names/colnames. tol (numeric(1)) Absolute tolerance used detect equality adjacent time points.

title: Remove adjacent duplicate values — trim_duplicates
text: named list : x: numeric vector matrix duplicate adjacent time points removed. times: numeric vector retained time points.

title: Remove adjacent duplicate values — trim_duplicates
code: # remove adjacent duplicates from a survival probability vector surv = c(1, 1, 0.8, 0.8, 0.5, 0.5, 0.2) trim_duplicates(surv, times = 1:7) #> $x #> [1] 1.0 0.8 0.5 0.2 #>  #> $times #> [1] 1 3 5 7 #>

title: survdistr 0.0.2
text: Added convert_to_surv() efficient transformation hazard/density functions survival anchor time points (uses C++ internally). Introduced trim_duplicates() remove repeated S(t) values across time axis, tolerance. Added interp_cif() constant interpolation CIF (uses C++ internally). Added extract_times() consistently obtain validate time points vectors matrices. Unified assertion function one: assert_prob(). Unified mat_interp() vec_interp() single interp() function. Added aliases three interpolation options (e.g., const_haz equivalent exp_surv). Implemented S(t) interpolation C++ 3 options (output = "surv"); H(t) F(t) follow similarly. Implemented f(t) interpolation C++ 3 options (output = "density") Implemented h(t) interpolation C++ 3 options (output = "hazard") Requires unique ordered eval_times, resulting significant C++ speed-. Allows passing times separately preserve precision. Removed data_type argument; input now S(t). Added aliases three interpolation options. Allows passing times construction separately precision. Introduced filter() method -place object filtering. Added rows argument filter input matrix interpolation without altering object. method times now active fields, public members. Added S3 conversion via as_survDistr(x). Added unit tests features.

title: survdistr 0.0.1
text: Base survDistr class. assert_prob_matrix() asserting survival, CDF CIF matrices. mat_interp() vec_interp() constant linear interpolation survival, CDF CIF matrices/vectors (using internal Rcpp methods).
